title: install.ps1: node version check breaks in Windows PowerShell 5.1 (embedded quotes stripped)
action: Replace the `node -p 'process.versions.node.split(".")[0]'` eval (PS 5.1 strips embedded double quotes when passing args to native exes, node receives split(.)[0] -> SyntaxError -> $nodeMajor=0 -> false 'Node.js 18+ required' failure even on v24) with pure PowerShell parsing of `node --version`: [int]((node --version).TrimStart('v').Split('.')[0]). Confirm install.sh does not have the same class of bug (it doesn't: bash preserves the quotes).
verify: grep -Fq "TrimStart('v')" scripts/install.ps1 && ! grep -Fq 'node -p' scripts/install.ps1 && /mnt/c/Windows/System32/WindowsPowerShell/v1.0/powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command "\$m = [int]((node --version).TrimStart('v').Split('.')[0]); if (\$m -lt 18) { exit 1 }; \"major=\$m\""

trace.outcome: install.ps1 works under Windows PowerShell 5.1: version check no longer false-fails, UNC/provider paths resolved, final status parse fixed
trace.verify_output: STATIC-CHECKS=PASS; PS 5.1 (5.1.26100.8737) end-to-end greenfield install completed: 'verify onboarding ok (bee 0.1.37), phase: idle' + 'bee installed.' in E:\Temp\bee-verify-ps; tree identical to install.sh route (only heading+timestamps differ)
trace.verify_command: static greps (no node -p eval, 3x Resolve-Path .ProviderPath, regex version parse, json join) + PS 5.1 end-to-end: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass install.ps1 -Directory %TEMP%\bee-verify-ps -Yes
